name: Kaimuki Crack Seed Store
tagline: Oahu's oldest crack seed shop — 80+ years of li hing mui, dried plum, and the snack culture that defines growing up local.
mustOrder: li hing mui Icee — the signature, sweet-sour-salty frozen perfection
crack seed by the pound — let the staff guide you if you're new
li hing gummy bears — the gateway drug for mainlanders
parking: Street parking on Koko Head Ave
talkStory: This unassuming shop on Koko Head Ave has been selling crack seed since the 1940s, making it one of the oldest in Hawaii. Crack seed — preserved dried fruits seasoned with li hing mui powder, salt, sugar, and spices — is a snack tradition brought by Chinese immigrants that became uniquely Hawaiian. Every local kid grew up with a bag of sweet-sour seed from a shop like this. 'Uncle' Kon Ping Young ran the store for 41 years before retiring in 2019; new owners kept the legacy alive. If you didn't grow up here, ask the staff what to try — they'll steer you right. The li hing Icees alone are worth the detour.
